integration_notes: The current candidate already meets target with very little accuracy headroom, so accuracy safety dominates. Focus edits inside train/eval timing boundaries rather than broad recipe changes. Any optimization that alters augmentation strength, schedule length, EMA behavior, or architecture capacity should be treated as high risk unless offset by a validated accuracy buffer. Keep warmup behavior aligned with compilation needs so measured trials stay fast.
performance_task: Prioritize low-risk speedups in the measured path: reduce evaluation/TTA overhead, remove unnecessary synchronizations, tighten dataloader/device transfer behavior, and inspect whether model forward or augmentation kernels can be fused/simplified without changing training dynamics materially. Only consider model changes if they are compile-friendly and have strong evidence of equal-or-better accuracy at the same epoch budget.
reliability_task: Harden the run contract and numerical stability: keep preflight cheap but representative, preserve dtype consistency for compiled mixed-precision convolutions and normalization, ensure JSON final line always emits even on edge paths, and remove or safely handle any torch.load usage that triggers warnings or could introduce runtime fragility.
round_goal: Trim measured trial time below the current ~2.59s while preserving the narrow but sufficient 94.02% TTA accuracy margin; avoid risky accuracy regressions.
